title: This may sound dumbbut what year did EVA come to the US
display_title: This may sound dumbbut what year did EVA come to the US
wikitext:
[]: This may sound dumb...but what year did EVA come to the U.S?             Quote Post by Kaworu-chan » Sat Mar 06, 2010 12:24 pm I know EVA debuted in Japan in 1995 but when did it debut in the U.S?

[]: Re: This may sound dumb...but what year did EVA come to the             Quote Post by Quiddity » Sat Mar 06, 2010 12:49 pm Kaworu-chan wrote: I know EVA debuted in Japan in 1995 but when did it debut in the U.S?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Mar 06, 2010 1:14 pm According to Amazon, the first VHS tape of Evangelion was released November 11, 1998. http://www.amazon.com/Neon-Genesis-Evangelion-Sub-VHS/dp/6304123531/ http://www.amazon.com/Neon-Genesis-Evangelion-Dub-VHS/dp/6304123523/ However, after looking up subsequent volumes of the TV show on VHS from ADV, it would appear they've all been dated November 11, 1998, which is most certainly erroneous. My next stop takes me to IMDB, where it states that it was released July 7, 1998. http://www.imdb.com/title/tt0112159/releaseinfo This seams similar, but it didn't sound quite right, because I was fairly certain that the films hadn't been released when ADV was beginning work on the series. And I remember 1996 being the year, based upon the fact that ADV actually caught up to the Japanese video release, and eventually used TV (ON AIR) masters rather than the video version masters. But then I went to the Evangelion Mailing List to check the archives from July 1998, and found this: http://eva.onegeek.org/pipermail/oldeva/1998-July/017164.html Which would indicate that the entire series, or at least up to #12, the next to last tape, had been released by that point. Then I remembered Brendan of Eva Otaku's bio on his website: http://web.archive.org/web/20030201093546/www.evaotaku.com/html/webmaster.html This would date his exposure to Evangelion somewhere in August-December of 96, probably earlier due to the fact that his Birthday is in March (we were born a day apart, how weird is that?) and that he bought the first tape with a gift card from his birthday. I remember reading that ADV was in license negotiations during Evangelion's original airing, so I believe they began releasing shortly after the series concluded in Japan. I tried finding something relevant in the old archives of the Evangelion ML, but nothing caught my eye: http://eva.onegeek.org/pipermail/oldeva/ The best I can determine is that the first volume came out sometime in mid-1996, and that the tapes were released through 1998. My Google-fu is rather weak right now, and there aren't too many really oldschool fans amongst us here (I don't think), I'm one of the more older fans, but I only go back to 1999. If you're really curious, you could drop Carl Horn, or Brendan Jamieson a PM, maybe they have that bit of info, they have accounts here: Carl: http://forum.evageeks.org/profile.php?mode=viewprofile&u=164 Brendan http://forum.evageeks.org/profile.php?mode=viewprofile&u=239 Alternatively, there are some oldschool fans on the Evangelion mailing list that might be able to fill you in: http://eva.onegeek.org/ Otherwise, I say sit back, and see if anyone has some mighty awesome Google-fu skills that can narrow it down to the actual month or day. I wonder if the release date is actually on the tape? If that were true, and my tapes weren't at home in storage, I could check. Oh well.

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Sat Mar 06, 2010 1:40 pm I believe it was released sometime back in early 97 IIRC? I think EoE was released in the USA back in 99 or 2000?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Mar 06, 2010 2:14 pm Guyver Spawn wrote: I believe it was released sometime back in early 97 IIRC? I think EoE was released in the USA back in 99 or 2000?
